principles rules stretches study projects setup

/home

I use this space to document how I design reliable platforms — principles, rules, and study notes captured as they evolve. Each entry is a practical checkpoint from building scalable systems, reminding me why certain approaches work and how to sharpen them. It stays reflective yet intentional, pushing me to keep learning and share what actually helps with delivery.

github · linkedin

achievements

  • Designed an internal Region Services PoC portal that automated request intake and streamlined regional support
  • Built compliant CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure patterns for the portal, aligned to Region Services security and deployment constraints
  • Optimised those pipelines to raise deployment reliability and reduce release risk
  • Integrated New Relic monitoring with OpenSearch tracing to speed incident response and root-cause analysis
  • Led operational support and weekly readiness reviews for an Amazon Redshift sub-service
  • Introduced Amazon Q AI-assisted diagnostics to shrink root-cause analysis from hours to minutes
  • Authored and maintained deployment process guides to standardise releases, onboarding, and incident response
  • Supported 24/7 global operations across 25+ international on-premises deployments
  • Migrated critical workloads from Microsoft Azure VMs to Amazon EC2 with near-zero downtime
  • Drove AWS and Azure cost optimisation initiatives that delivered meaningful infrastructure savings
  • Created SOPs and disaster recovery runbooks to harden operational resilience
  • Rewrote and standardised Redshift runbooks, cutting onboarding and resolution time
  • Deployed and managed 130+ Azure IoT Hub edge modules, improving remote infrastructure reliability

key skills

  • Amazon Web Services (AWS) architecture
  • Microsoft Azure hybrid workloads

  • Terraform infrastructure as code
  • AWS CDK patterns
  • Jenkins orchestration
  • Octopus Deploy release automation

  • Docker container delivery

  • PowerShell operational automation
